spoken_briefing:
Today in AI tools for Modern Contractors. It's Monday, June 22, 2026. Let's go through every category, every tool, and what's changed.

Starting with Bidding and Estimating.

STACK from STACK Construction Technologies is a cloud-based estimating platform for trade subcontractors with AI-assisted takeoff and bid management. Pricing is around two thousand nine hundred and ninety-nine dollars per year. Independent testing found STACK delivered accuracy within three percent of baseline on complex commercial projects.

Togal dot A I from Togal AI is an AI-driven floor plan takeoff platform claiming ninety-eight percent accuracy. Pricing starts at one hundred ninety-nine dollars per user per month. Coastal Construction saved fourteen and a half hours per plan set, about one million dollars in annual cost savings.

BidIntell from BidIntell is an AI pre-bid decision platform that scores incoming bid invitations on a hundred-point BidIndex to help subs decide which opportunities are worth pursuing. Pricing is forty-nine dollars per user per month. It reduces wasted estimating hours on low-probability bids.

Beam AI from Attentive dot A I is an AI estimating and bid management platform for commercial specialty contractors with automated takeoff for mechanical, plumbing, HVAC, and structural steel. Pricing starts around two hundred ninety-nine dollars per month. The platform has completed five hundred thousand takeoffs and reportedly saved twenty million hours of manual estimating.

Downtobid from Downtobid reads uploaded plans, identifies trade scopes, and sends targeted bid invitations to relevant subcontractors. Pricing starts at nine hundred and ninety-nine dollars per month. It generates scopes in minutes versus days and delivers two times more bids per invitation.

ConstructConnect Takeoff Boost from ConstructConnect partners with Togal AI to bring AI-assisted takeoff into the ConstructConnect platform. Pricing is custom. It's integrated into ConstructConnect's existing bid board for GCs and large subs.

Drawer AI from Drawer AI uses computer vision to interpret construction plans and extract structured data for estimating. Pricing is custom. It automates plan reading to dramatically reduce takeoff time.

TaksoAI from TaksoAI provides AI-assisted quantity takeoff and estimating for general and specialty contractors. Pricing starts around one hundred forty-nine dollars per month. It integrates with Procore and Autodesk for seamless data flow.

XBuild from XBuild is an AI-powered estimating platform using natural language processing to convert scope descriptions into detailed estimates. Pricing is custom. Contractors report sixty percent faster bid preparation using the platform.

Trimble LiveCount AI Automate Takeoff from Trimble uses machine learning to automatically count and measure items in construction drawings. Pricing is bundled with Trimble solutions or available standalone. It integrates natively with Trimble's Estimation and Accubid products.

Quotr dot A I from Quotr is a full preconstruction operating system with AI-assisted quantity takeoff, computer vision mapping, and a network of two hundred twenty plus vetted factories for direct materials procurement at forty to fifty percent below retail markup. It reduces estimation time by ninety percent.

Space AI from Space AI provides automated space measurement and area calculation from architectural drawings. Pricing is custom enterprise. It delivers sub-one-percent measurement accuracy.

ConstructionBids dot A I from ConstructionBids AI is an AI bid generation platform that analyzes project documents and drafts complete bid packages. Contractors report sixty percent faster bid prep time.

Kreo from Kreo Software is a cloud-based estimating and cost planning platform with AI-driven quantity takeoff from BIM models. Pricing starts around two hundred dollars per user per month. It integrates with Revit and other BIM platforms.

Fresco from Fresco is an AI estimating copilot for specialty contractors that uses historical project data to improve estimate accuracy over time. Pricing is custom. Teams using Fresco report fifty percent faster estimates.

Rudus from Rudus is an AI-powered materials estimating and procurement platform for concrete and masonry subcontractors. Pricing is custom. It connects estimates directly to supplier pricing and availability.

Bidi Contracting from Bidi Contracting is an AI-assisted bidding workflow tool for general contractors managing multi-trade bids. Pricing is subscription-based. It streamlines the GC bid management process from invitation to award.

BuildVision AI from BuildVision AI provides AI-powered procurement and project tracking for GCs and owners. Pricing is custom. It monitors real-time supply chain pricing to help capture savings during preconstruction.

Mercator dot AI from Mercator AI is a construction intelligence platform that identifies upcoming construction projects and scores them by win probability for contractors. Pricing starts around three hundred dollars per month. It helps business development teams find and prioritize the right opportunities.

Bidflow from Bidflow is an AI-powered bid management platform for trade contractors tracking incoming GC invitations. Pricing is custom. It centralizes bid invitations across multiple GC portals into one workflow.

QuoteGoat from QuoteGoat is an AI-assisted estimating tool for small and mid-size contractors generating quick preliminary quotes. Pricing starts at forty-nine dollars per month. It converts project descriptions into preliminary line-item estimates.

CostKit from CostKit is an AI construction cost estimating platform built around historical job cost data. Pricing is custom. It helps contractors build more accurate estimates based on their own historical performance.

Handoff AI from Handoff is an AI platform that converts construction documents into structured scope sheets and bid forms for subcontractors. Pricing starts around ninety-nine dollars per month. It reduces the time to prepare and send bid packages from hours to minutes.

PLAN0 AI from PLAN0 is an AI-native preconstruction tool that reads project specifications and drawings to automatically generate trade-specific scope sheets. Pricing is custom. It eliminates manual spec reading from the estimating workflow.

And new this week, Archdesk from Archdesk is a cloud-based all-in-one construction management platform combining estimating, scheduling, budgeting, procurement, and document management, modeling over one hundred eighty entity types and serving as a strong data foundation for AI tool layering. Pricing starts around two hundred ninety-nine dollars per month. Over ten thousand active users across six continents are managing two hundred billion dollars in collective projects on the platform.

Moving to Submittal and RFI Tracking.

Trunk Tools from Trunk Tools is an AI platform that organizes project documents and answers natural language queries with cited references. On June seventeenth, twenty twenty-six, Trunk Tools launched Cortex, the brain of construction AI, built over four years from live GC project data. Cortex powers five agents: TrunkReview for drawing revision analysis, TrunkBrowse for contextual data retrieval, TrunkRFI for preventing unnecessary RFIs and drafting needed ones, TrunkBid for apples-to-apples bid comparison, and TrunkRegister which reads the entire spec book, builds a submittal register, and writes it back to your project management system in minutes, eliminating thirty or more hours of preconstruction work per project. Total funding is seventy million dollars.

Procore AI from Procore Technologies, powered by the Datagrid intelligence layer acquired in January twenty twenty-six, includes five prebuilt agents: Submittal Review, RFI, Deep Search, Daily Log, and Contract Review. On June first, twenty twenty-six, Procore relaunched its Common Data Environment with agentic AI at its core, and launched Agentic APIs allowing third-party AI systems to read and write to Procore. A NVIDIA Omniverse partnership enables digital twin capabilities for AI factories and data centers. Agent Builder is now in open beta for all customers. Buro Happold is on track to reduce construction administration by fifty percent using Procore AI agents.

Civils dot AI from Civils AI automates document management, plan review, and RFI tracking for civil construction projects. Pricing is custom. It reduces document review time by up to seventy percent.

BuildSync from BuildSync is an AI-powered submittal and document compliance platform. Pricing is custom. AI compliance verification reduces submittal rejection rates from thirty-five percent down to five percent.

SubmittalLink from SubmittalLink is a cloud-based submittal tracking and approval platform with AI routing and deadline management. Pricing starts around two hundred dollars per month. It reduces submittal cycle time by forty percent.

Constructable from Constructable is an AI-powered construction document management platform with intelligent search and automated RFI generation. Pricing is custom. It provides construction-specific AI search across all project documents.

Nomic AI from Nomic provides AI-powered embeddings and search for large document collections, used by construction teams to make project archives searchable. Pricing starts at free with paid API tiers. It enables semantic search across thousands of project documents.

Articulate from Articulate is an AI document review and summarization platform used in construction for RFI and submittal analysis. Pricing is custom. It reduces time spent reviewing lengthy technical documents by up to sixty percent.

Inventive AI from Inventive AI is an AI platform for RFI and submittal workflow automation with intelligent routing and status tracking. Pricing is custom. It reduces RFI response time significantly through automated routing and draft generation.

AutoRFP dot A I from AutoRFP AI generates RFI responses in seconds by searching approved content and past responses. Pricing starts at custom enterprise tiers. It cuts RFI response drafting time from days to minutes.

And new this week, Ruh AI from Ruh AI is an AI agent platform that deploys AI employees to handle RFI routing, analysis, and draft responses with full paper trail, while also covering estimates, daily logs, vendor onboarding, and invoicing, with three thousand plus integrations and no replacement of existing tools required. RFI response time drops from five to ten days down to twenty-four hours, saving sixty or more coordinator hours per month, and preventing fifty thousand or more dollars in daily crew idle costs.

Now Field Management and Daily Reporting.

CompanyCam from CompanyCam is a photo documentation and project communication platform for contractors with AI-powered photo organization and reporting. Pricing starts at thirty-nine dollars per user per month. Over two hundred thousand contractors use it for real-time site documentation.

eSUB from eSUB is a subcontractor project management platform with AI daily reports, document management, and change order tracking. Pricing is custom. Subcontractors using eSUB report forty percent reduction in admin time.

Buildots from Buildots uses three-sixty cameras on hardhats to automatically compare actual construction progress against schedule. In May twenty twenty-five, Buildots raised forty-five million dollars in Series D, totaling one hundred sixty-six million dollars raised. In October twenty twenty-five they acquired Genda for workforce and safety management. Projects report up to fifty percent reduction in delays. Customers include DPR, Hensel Phelps, Clark Construction, and JE Dunn.

Raken from Raken, backed by Sverica Capital, specializes in mobile field reporting with voice-to-report, photo documentation, and daily log templates. Pricing starts at fifteen dollars per user per month. Over five thousand construction companies use Raken and report fifty percent faster daily report completion.

BuildOps from BuildOps is a specialty contractor operations platform with AI-assisted dispatching, time tracking, and job costing. Pricing is custom enterprise. Specialty contractors report thirty percent increase in billable hours captured.

InspectMind AI from InspectMind AI generates structured field reports from photos and voice notes using AI. Pricing starts at forty-nine dollars per month. It cuts field report preparation time by eighty percent.

iFieldSmart from iFieldSmart Technologies is a construction field management platform with AI-powered drawing management, RFI tracking, and daily reports. Pricing is custom. It provides real-time field-to-office data synchronization.

Kahua from Kahua is an enterprise construction project management platform with AI-assisted document management and workflow automation. Pricing is custom. Large GCs and owners use Kahua for multi-project portfolio management.

OpenSpace from OpenSpace uses three-sixty cameras to automatically capture and map construction progress, creating a digital twin of the jobsite. Pricing starts at custom enterprise. It delivers automated progress documentation across hundreds of simultaneous projects.

Novade from Novade Solutions is a mobile-first construction field management platform for quality, safety, and daily operations. Pricing is custom. Asia-Pacific contractors report sixty percent faster site inspections.

Benetics AI from Benetics AI uses AI to analyze workforce productivity patterns and optimize crew deployment on construction sites. Pricing is custom. Early adopters report fifteen to twenty percent improvement in crew productivity.

BuilderHelp from BuilderHelp is an AI-powered customer communication and project update platform for home builders and remodelers. Pricing starts at ninety-nine dollars per month. It automates client update generation and reduces incoming calls by thirty percent.

KYRO AI from KYRO AI is a construction management platform built for vegetation management, storm restoration, and construction with AI-powered field communication and invoicing. Pricing is custom. It streamlines field documentation and billing for specialized restoration crews.

Klutch AI from Klutch AI provides AI-powered project management assistance for construction teams with intelligent scheduling and daily log automation. Pricing starts at custom enterprise. It reduces project management administrative overhead by forty percent.

Opusense AI from Opusense AI is an AI field operations platform with automated progress tracking and resource optimization. Pricing is custom. It provides real-time visibility into field operations for both GCs and specialty subs.

Hardline from Hardline is a construction field management platform with AI-assisted punch list generation and quality control workflows. Pricing is custom. It reduces punch list completion time by fifty percent.

Track3D from Track3D uses three-dimensional scanning and AI to automatically track construction progress and generate as-built documentation. Pricing is custom. It delivers millimeter-accurate progress documentation.

Doxel AI from Doxel uses cameras to automate progress and schedule tracking for all trades. Pricing is custom enterprise. Named one of Forbes' Best Startup Employers in America in March twenty twenty-six. Projects report significant schedule deviation detection weeks earlier than traditional methods.

NavigateAI from NavigateAI is an AI construction management platform providing predictive insights for project outcomes. Pricing is custom. It identifies schedule risks before they become delays.

SMRT-E from SMARTBUILD Construction Solutions is a smart building and field management platform with AI-integrated IoT sensors and mobile reporting. Pricing is custom. It connects field equipment, labor, and materials tracking in real time.

Fieldwire by Hilti from Hilti is a mobile-first field management platform for crew task management, drawing access, and daily reporting with AI features for daily report generation. Pricing starts at fifty-four dollars per user per month. Over one million construction professionals use Fieldwire globally.

And new this week, Pillar AI Operating System from Pillar, a recently funded Italian startup, is an AI operating system for construction contractors that consolidates accounting data, bank feeds, and job site updates including WhatsApp messages from crews into one real-time interface. The company raised twelve million euros in seed funding in May twenty twenty-six led by Earlybird and Base10 Partners. In the first four months of twenty twenty-six alone, Pillar hit one million euros in ARR with four hundred active contractors, processed four hundred sixty thousand invoices, and covered five thousand seven hundred job sites. Clients save an average of fourteen hours per week on administrative tasks.

Now T and M and Billing Automation.

HCSS HeavyJob from HCSS is a field data collection and job cost management platform for heavy civil contractors with T and M billing integration. Pricing is custom enterprise. Heavy civil contractors report thirty percent reduction in payroll processing time.

FOUNDATION T and M Billing Module from FOUNDATION Software is a construction accounting platform with automated markup, customizable invoices, and job cost integration for T and M work. Pricing is custom. It eliminates manual T and M invoice calculation for contractors doing mixed contract types.

SmartBarrel from SmartBarrel uses facial recognition and biometric technology to automate workforce time capture and T and M reporting on construction sites. Pricing is custom enterprise. Contractors report fifty percent reduction in payroll processing time and elimination of buddy punching.

Rhumbix from Rhumbix, acquired by Autodesk with the acquisition closing March thirty-first, twenty twenty-six, is a T and M billing and field time tracking platform now integrated into the Autodesk Construction Cloud ecosystem. Pricing is part of Autodesk bundle. The platform continues to operate and integrates natively with Autodesk's workflows.

Siteline from Siteline is a pay application and billing automation platform for specialty subcontractors. Pricing starts at four hundred ninety-nine dollars per month. Subs using Siteline report getting paid thirty percent faster and cutting billing preparation time by seventy-five percent.

Adaptive from Adaptive is an AI-powered financial planning and job costing platform for construction companies replacing spreadsheet-based budgeting. Pricing is custom. Project managers report forty percent improvement in budget forecast accuracy.

SubBase from SubBase is a subcontractor management and payment platform streamlining compliance, onboarding, and invoice processing. Pricing is custom. GCs report forty percent reduction in subcontractor payment processing time.

Payra from Payra is an AI-powered accounts payable automation platform for construction companies processing invoices and payments. Pricing starts at three hundred dollars per month. Contractors report seventy percent reduction in invoice processing time.

Intuit Enterprise Suite Construction Edition from Intuit is a construction-specific financial management platform built on the QuickBooks architecture with job costing, payroll, and T and M billing. Pricing is custom enterprise. It brings enterprise accounting capabilities to mid-market contractors.

Trimble Pay from Trimble is a T and M and schedule-of-values billing platform integrated with Trimble's construction ecosystem. Pricing is bundled with Trimble solutions. It automates B2W Track field data into billing workflows.

Trayd from Trayd is a construction payroll and time tracking platform with T and M billing capabilities built for specialty contractors. Pricing starts at two hundred ninety-nine dollars per month. It integrates field time directly into payroll and billing.

inBuild from inBuild is an AI-powered construction financial platform for real-time job cost tracking and invoice automation. Pricing is custom. It provides real-time financial visibility across multiple projects simultaneously.

Field Materials from Field Materials AI is an AI platform for construction material management, purchase order automation, and field delivery tracking. Pricing is custom. It reduces material cost overruns by flagging discrepancies between ordered and delivered quantities.

BlueCollar from BlueCollar is a construction workforce and job costing platform with mobile time capture and T and M billing integration. Pricing is custom. It automates labor cost allocation from field capture to billing.

Earlytrade from Earlytrade is an early payment financing platform for construction subcontractors, offering accelerated payment terms in exchange for a discount. Pricing is based on financing rates. Subs using Earlytrade improve cash flow by accessing payment in days rather than weeks.

Werx from Werx is a T and M billing and job management platform for field service and construction contractors. Pricing starts at forty-nine dollars per month. It generates T and M invoices directly from field time entries.

Vergo from Vergo is a construction financial management platform with AI-powered cost forecasting and billing automation. Pricing is custom. It delivers real-time cost-to-complete projections for active projects.

Kaya AI from Kaya AI is an AI assistant for construction project management covering budget tracking, change order management, and reporting. Pricing is custom. It reduces time spent on financial reporting by fifty percent.

Buildern from Buildern is a construction management platform for residential builders with estimating, scheduling, and billing automation. Pricing starts at ninety-nine dollars per month. Builders report twenty percent decrease in billing manual workload.

Factura dot AI from Factura AI is an AI-powered invoice processing and accounts payable automation platform used by construction companies for high-volume invoice handling. Pricing is custom. It processes invoices with ninety-eight percent accuracy, eliminating manual data entry.

Vic dot AI from Vic AI is an autonomous accounts payable platform using AI to process, code, and approve invoices without human intervention. Pricing is custom enterprise. Vic AI customers report seventy percent reduction in invoice processing cost.

GCPay from Autodesk, formerly GCPay and now an Autodesk company, is a subcontractor billing and pay application platform integrated into Autodesk Construction Cloud. Pricing is custom. It streamlines the pay application process between GCs and subcontractors.

Briq from Briq is a financial automation platform for construction companies covering budgeting, forecasting, and cash flow management with AI-driven insights. Pricing is custom enterprise. Contractors report forty percent reduction in time spent on financial reporting.

Three new tools this week in T and M and Billing.

KonstructIQ from KonstructIQ is an AI construction management platform for remodelers and builders that generates detailed line-item estimates with labor and materials from a brief job description in minutes, then converts approved estimates directly into budgets with embedded payment systems for vendors and subs. Pricing is one hundred ninety-nine dollars per month with unlimited projects and users and no contracts. Cuts estimation time from days to minutes.

Assignar Pay from Assignar is an AI-powered T and M to invoice platform that reads rate card PDFs, interprets every billing condition including travel, minimums, and allowances, and auto-matches approved field time entries to generate invoices instantly. Clients save an average of three workdays every month.

Premier Construction Software from Premier Construction Software is a global AI-powered construction ERP for growing general contractors, consolidating accounting, project management, and field tools with AI-powered AP automation and voice-activated job logs. Pricing runs from one hundred twenty-five to three hundred forty-nine dollars per user per month. Expert implementation targets sixty-day go-live.

Now Change Order and Document Management.

Document Crunch, acquired by Trimble in April twenty twenty-six, is an AI contract analysis platform that reads construction contracts, highlights risky clauses, and flags unusual risk shifting provisions. It now integrates across the Trimble Construction One ecosystem.

Procore Change Order Management with AI Assist from Procore Technologies provides AI-powered change order preparation within Procore, with agents for scope gap identification, draft language generation, and financial impact tracking. It's native to the Procore platform.

Pelles AI from Pelles AI is a generative AI platform for construction document creation, including proposals, RFIs, submittal logs, and change orders. Pricing is custom. It automates document-heavy preconstruction and project admin tasks.

Clearstory from Clearstory is a change order management platform with real-time GC to sub collaboration. In March twenty twenty-six, Clearstory launched AI Reason Categories that auto-tag every change order on entry and can backfill thousands of existing CORs. In May twenty twenty-six, Clearstory AI launched four connected agents: change notification, pricing, review, and compliance. The COR Pricing Agent entered closed beta May twenty-eighth, allowing contractors to describe a change in plain language and have AI draft the COR and suggest pricing. Turner Construction has rolled out Clearstory platform-wide. The dataset covers one point three million change orders processing three point five billion dollars monthly. Users report sixty-five percent productivity boost and seven to ten hours per week saved per person.

Bluebeam Max from Bluebeam is an advanced construction document markup, collaboration, and workflow platform with AI-enhanced review features. Pricing starts at eighty dollars per user per month. Over three million construction professionals use Bluebeam for plan review.

Structured AI from Structured AI uses AI to extract and structure data from construction documents, contracts, and specifications. Pricing is custom. It transforms unstructured construction documents into searchable, actionable data.

Buildcheck from Buildcheck is an AI document review platform for construction that checks drawings and specifications for compliance issues before submission. Pricing is custom. It identifies code compliance issues before they become costly corrections.

Billy from Billy is an insurance and compliance certificate management platform for construction companies with AI-automated certificate tracking. Pricing starts at ninety-nine dollars per month. Contractors report sixty percent reduction in time managing subcontractor compliance certificates.

LightTable from LightTable is an AI-powered construction document comparison tool for identifying changes between drawing revisions. Pricing is custom. It finds changes in minutes versus hours of manual comparison.

Spellbook from Spellbook is an AI contract review and drafting platform that identifies risky clauses and suggests alternative language. Pricing starts at ninety-nine dollars per user per month. Legal and operations teams report fifty percent faster contract review.

Rossum, now part of Coupa, acquired by Coupa on May twelfth, twenty twenty-six, is an AI document data extraction platform that processes invoices, purchase orders, and construction billing documents. It is now integrated within Coupa's procurement and spend management ecosystem.

Moving to Proposal Generation and Pre-Bid Analysis.

BidIntell from BidIntell also appears in this category as an AI pre-bid scoring and qualification tool helping specialty contractors evaluate bid opportunities before committing estimating resources. Pricing is forty-nine dollars per user per month.

Flowcase from Flowcase is an AI-powered proposal and case study management platform helping contractors compile winning submissions with reusable content libraries. Pricing is custom enterprise. Teams report fifty percent faster proposal assembly.

ContraVault from ContraVault is an AI construction bidding platform covering opportunity qualification, proposal creation, subcontractor coordination, compliance analysis, and risk detection for GCs and EPC firms. Pricing is custom. It provides end-to-end preconstruction workflow in one platform.

ConCntric from ConCntric is an AI-powered prebid and preconstruction platform with scope analysis, subcontractor bid management, and budget tracking. Pricing is custom. GCs report thirty percent improvement in bid accuracy using ConCntric's historical data layer.

Provision from Provision is an AI estimating and project management platform for specialty contractors with scope analysis and bid preparation tools. Pricing is custom. Contractors report forty percent faster estimate preparation.

MeltPlan from MeltPlan is an AI bid leveling and scope comparison tool for GCs that normalizes subcontractor proposals to a common baseline. Pricing is custom. It catches scope gaps in minutes that would otherwise take hours of manual comparison.

LeanCon from LeanCon is an AI-powered lean construction planning tool with prebid analysis and project delivery optimization features. Pricing is custom. Teams using LeanCon report fifteen to twenty percent schedule compression on complex projects.

Brickanta from Brickanta is an AI construction project intelligence platform covering prebid research, market analysis, and competitor tracking for contractors. Pricing is custom. It provides AI-driven go or no-go scoring for incoming bid opportunities.

VisibleThread from VisibleThread is an AI proposal analysis tool that improves clarity and compliance by identifying gaps, unclear language, and missing requirements. Pricing starts at custom enterprise. It helps teams submit stronger, more defensible proposals.

Two new tools this week in Proposal Generation.

Buildr from Buildr is an AI preconstruction workspace built for GCs handling ten million to one hundred million dollars annually, with AI bid leveling that flags scope gaps before buyout, estimating from historical cost models, a CRM with pipeline tracking and relationship scoring, and workforce planning tied to pipeline probability. Pricing is custom. AI bid analysis adoption among GCs grew three hundred forty percent between twenty twenty-four and twenty twenty-six.

DeepRFP from DeepRFP is an AI RFP response platform enabling teams to write proposals eighty percent faster through agents for proposal writing, compliance matrix generation, questionnaire response, and RFP shredding, with source citations and confidence scores on every AI-generated response. Pricing is seventy-five dollars per user per month for Pro and one hundred twenty-five dollars for Elite. A seven-day free trial is available with no credit card required.

Now Project Scheduling with AI.

ALICE Technologies from ALICE Technologies is a generative AI construction scheduling platform that simulates and compares a wide range of project schedules to optimize resource sequencing. Pricing is custom. McKinsey documented a forty percent construction schedule reduction on a data center project using ALICE.

Phoenix Project Manager from Phoenix Project Manager is a CPM scheduling software with advanced resource leveling and what-if analysis for complex construction projects. Pricing starts at twelve hundred dollars per year. It's used by government and infrastructure contractors for complex schedule management.

Oracle Primavera P6 from Oracle is the enterprise construction scheduling standard with AI-enhanced predictive analytics and portfolio risk management. Pricing is custom enterprise. It's the dominant platform for large infrastructure and EPC projects globally.

nPlan from nPlan is an AI scheduling platform trained on seven hundred fifty thousand historical schedules representing over two point five trillion dollars in capital spend. Products include Schedule Studio, which generates logic-linked schedules from scope text in minutes; Barry, the first AI assistant for project decision-making; nPlan Insights Pro for forecasting and risk per project; and nPlan Portfolio for systemic risk across multiple projects. nPlan raised sixteen million dollars in Series B in October twenty twenty-five. The platform has saved customers one point two billion dollars to date.

SmartPM from SmartPM Technologies is an AI-powered schedule analytics platform that evaluates CPM schedule quality and tracks compression, delay, and performance metrics. Pricing starts at five hundred dollars per month. Contractors report fifty percent reduction in schedule analysis time.

Outbuild from Outbuild is an AI-assisted lookahead scheduling platform for construction teams connecting master schedule milestones to weekly work plans. Pricing starts at two hundred dollars per month. Field teams using Outbuild report thirty percent improvement in schedule reliability.

Planera from Planera is an AI-powered project scheduling platform with natural language schedule creation and scenario modeling. Pricing is custom. It allows project teams to ask questions about their schedules in plain English and get instant answers.

Civalgo from Civalgo is an AI-driven construction management platform with scheduling, resource allocation, and daily reporting tools for civil contractors. Pricing is custom. Heavy civil contractors report twenty-five percent improvement in equipment utilization.

Mastt from Mastt is a capital project management platform with AI-powered reporting and schedule tracking for owner-side project controls. Pricing is custom. Infrastructure owners report sixty percent reduction in reporting preparation time.

And finally, Safety and Compliance Monitoring.

Intenseye from Intenseye is a computer vision safety monitoring platform using existing CCTV cameras to detect PPE violations and unsafe behaviors in real time. Pricing is custom enterprise. Manufacturing and construction sites report forty to fifty percent reduction in recordable incidents.

Voxel AI from Voxel AI uses computer vision and machine learning on existing security cameras to identify workplace safety risks and ergonomic hazards. Pricing is custom. Early construction customers report thirty to forty percent reduction in injury rates.

Oracle Construction and Engineering Advisor for Safety from Oracle is an AI predictive intelligence solution that forecasts safety incidents before they occur by analyzing project data patterns. Pricing is part of Oracle Construction and Engineering suite. Announced in March twenty twenty-six.

Pillar Technologies from Pillar Technologies is a construction site environmental sensor platform monitoring temperature, moisture, atmospheric conditions, and visual hazards for concrete curing, air quality, and heat stress alerts. Pricing is custom. It provides early warning for environmental hazards on sensitive construction operations.

DroneDeploy Safety AI from DroneDeploy uses drone-captured imagery and AI to monitor construction site safety conditions and progress from aerial perspectives. Pricing starts at four hundred ninety-nine dollars per month. It provides weekly site-wide safety and progress documentation from the air.

viAct from viAct is a computer vision AI platform for construction safety monitoring covering PPE detection, confined space monitoring, and near-miss identification. Pricing is custom enterprise. Asia-Pacific and Middle East projects report forty percent reduction in safety violations.

Fyld from Fyld analyzes short video clips from jobsites to identify safety risks and quality issues before they escalate. Fyld reported eighty-two percent year-over-year growth in twenty twenty-five and expanded to Kiewit and Emery Sapp and Sons. Contractors using Fyld report reductions in serious workplace incidents of up to forty-eight percent.

Safety Evolution SE-AI from Safety Evolution is an AI-powered safety management platform covering hazard identification, incident tracking, and compliance reporting with predictive risk scoring. Pricing starts at custom enterprise. Clients report forty percent reduction in time spent on safety documentation.

Kwant dot AI from Kwant AI is an AI workforce intelligence platform for construction sites combining IoT badges and AI to track worker locations, productivity, and safety compliance in real time. Pricing is custom. Projects using Kwant report twenty-five percent improvement in workforce productivity.

Hakimo AI from Hakimo AI is an AI security and safety monitoring platform for construction sites using computer vision and real-time alert generation for access control and safety violations. Pricing is custom. Construction customers report seventy percent reduction in unauthorized site access incidents.

Sensera Systems from Sensera Systems provides solar-powered construction site cameras with AI-powered time-lapse, monitoring, and safety alerting capabilities. Pricing starts at three hundred dollars per month per camera. It delivers continuous site monitoring without requiring power infrastructure.

FieldFlō from FieldFlō is an AI field safety and compliance platform for specialty contractors with digital safety plans, incident reporting, and compliance tracking. Pricing starts at custom. It reduces safety documentation preparation time by sixty percent.

Newmetrix from Newmetrix, formerly Smartvid dot io, is an AI safety and risk management platform using computer vision and its Vinnie AI engine to analyze site photos and video for hazard identification and predictive risk scoring. Construction firms using Newmetrix report forty percent reductions in recordable incidents within the first year. The Vinnie AI engine can save construction companies an average of one point four to three point six million dollars per year through avoided safety incidents.

Field1st from Field1st is a mobile safety management platform for construction crews with AI-assisted safety observations, digital job hazard analyses, and real-time hazard reporting. Pricing starts at custom. Crews using Field1st report fifty percent faster safety observation completion.

Nyfty dot AI from Nyfty AI is an AI safety and compliance platform for construction sites covering permit tracking, safety training records, and real-time compliance alerts. Pricing is custom. It provides continuous compliance monitoring versus periodic manual audits.

Detect Technologies from Detect Technologies uses IoT sensors and AI to monitor construction and industrial sites for real-time hazard detection and predictive maintenance. Pricing is custom enterprise. Industrial and construction customers report thirty to forty percent reduction in unplanned downtime.

iFactory from iFactory AI is an AI-powered quality and safety inspection platform for construction sites using computer vision and mobile inspection tools. Pricing is custom. Teams using iFactory report sixty percent reduction in inspection time.

CompScience from CompScience is a workers' compensation AI platform that analyzes jobsite video to identify injury risks and provides insurance credits to contractors demonstrating safety improvement. Pricing is tied to workers' comp premiums. Contractors using CompScience receive premium credits of ten to twenty percent while reducing injury rates.

And new this week, Syntora from Syntora builds custom AI systems for construction compliance tracking and safety monitoring. Using natural language processing to scan daily reports and site photos for OSHA certification gaps and PPE violations, and computer vision for real-time hazard detection with alerts under ten seconds. For Procore-connected firms, a production-ready compliance monitor deploys in four weeks. Clients go from ten hours of manual review per week to under thirty minutes per day. Deployments go into the client's own AWS account with full source code provided.

Now a word on the broader market.

What owners and large GCs are expecting from Modern Contractors in twenty twenty-six: digital-first documentation is now standard. RFIs, submittals, and change orders must be tracked in software. Owners expect real-time project data access and AI-assisted change order transparency. Turner Construction has platform-wide requirements for digital change order management. The GSA proposed a significant AI contract clause in March twenty twenty-six requiring federal contractors to disclose all AI tools used, grant government data ownership over AI inputs and outputs, implement comprehensive security safeguards, and prohibit using government data to train AI models. Large GCs increasingly require subcontractors to use compatible digital tools as a condition of bidding. Construction AI adoption has doubled in twenty twenty-six as owners push for faster documentation, real-time cost visibility, and proactive safety reporting.

On realistic adoption costs for a Modern Contractor with five to twenty project managers: expect fifteen thousand to eighty thousand dollars per year covering estimating, field management, document management, and safety monitoring. Point solutions start at three thousand to six thousand per year. New single-platform options like KonstructIQ at one hundred ninety-nine dollars per month dramatically lower the entry point. Implementation and training typically add ten to twenty percent in year one.

On documented ROI: sixty percent faster bid prep time from ConstructionBids AI. Fourteen and a half hours saved per plan set at Coastal Construction with Togal. Thirty to fifty percent reduction in admin hours from automated field reports. Thirty-five to fifty percent incident rate reductions from AI safety platforms. nPlan has saved customers one point two billion dollars using AI scheduling. Trunk Tools TrunkRegister eliminates thirty or more preconstruction hours per project. Clearstory users report sixty-five percent productivity boost and seven to ten hours per week saved per person on change orders. Ruh AI cuts RFI response time from five to ten days to twenty-four hours. Pillar AI Operating System clients save fourteen hours per week on admin. Industry-wide, sixty-eight percent of early AI adopters saved at least fifty thousand dollars, and ROI on AI investments averaged three point five times within eighteen months for early construction adopters.

That's your AI briefing for Modern Contractors. Monday, June twenty-second, twenty twenty-six.

tools.name: Trunk Tools
tools.description: AI platform that organizes project documents (specs, RFIs, submittals, drawings, schedules) and answers natural-language queries with cited references. June 17, 2026: Launched Cortex, an intelligence layer built over four years from live construction data at major U.S. GCs — reads, interprets, and structures data from blueprints, specs, RFIs, submittals, schedules, and change orders. Cortex powers seven specialized agents: TrunkReview (drawing revision analysis with patent-pending change narrative generation, reviews a 20-sheet bulletin in under 5 minutes including changes the architect didn't cloud), TrunkBrowse (contextual project data retrieval enriching 2D drawings with full job context), TrunkRFI (prevents unnecessary RFIs, auto-drafts needed ones, provides trade and schedule impact analysis), TrunkBid (apples-to-apples bid comparison against scope requirements), TrunkRegister (reads entire spec book, builds submittal register, writes it to the project management system — eliminating 30+ hours of preconstruction work per project), TrunkSubmittal (71% faster submittal review, covers nearly every submittal type, automatically associates relevant specs and RFI responses — embedded directly in Procore and Autodesk Forma as a workflow step), and Schedule Agent (autonomously links scheduled activities to supporting documentation). Procore integration restored June 17, 2026 as part of Cortex launch (had been removed September 2025). Raised $40M Series B (July 2025, led by Insight Partners); total funding $70M.

tools.results: Reduces document retrieval time from hours to seconds. TrunkRegister eliminates 30+ hours of preconstruction work per project. Schedule Agent creates dynamic links between scheduled activities and all essential supporting documentation. TrunkRFI reduces unnecessary RFIs and provides trade/schedule impact analysis. TrunkBid accelerates buyout through automated scope comparison.

tools.spoken: Trunk Tools from Trunk Tools is an AI platform that organizes project documents and answers natural language queries with cited references. Cortex powers five agents: TrunkReview for drawing revision analysis, TrunkBrowse for contextual data retrieval, TrunkRFI for preventing unnecessary RFIs and drafting needed ones, TrunkBid for apples-to-apples bid comparison, and TrunkRegister which reads the entire spec book, builds a submittal register, and writes it back to your project management system in minutes, eliminating thirty or more hours of preconstruction work per project.
