tools.integrations: Not publicly documented; platform is standalone with API for custom connections
tools.spoken: StruxHub from StruxHub is a cloud construction site operations platform built for general contractors and superintendents that puts deliveries, logistics, safety forms, and resource tracking on a live geolocated map. Pricing is custom. Clients report eighty-three percent reduction in delivery coordination time.

tools.name: HCSS HeavyJob
tools.description: AI-enhanced field operations and cost management platform for heavy civil and specialty contractors. 2026 Winter Release added HCSS Copilot AI: summarizes the Daily Digest, helps users navigate the platform, and answers questions using company knowledge base and job data. 2026 Spring Release added an AI-powered Summarize tool for audit logs, direct HeavyBid-to-HeavyJob estimate handoff, and Docs and Plans integrated directly into the HeavyJob mobile app. Acquisition pending: Nemetschek Group announced a $2.4B agreement to acquire HCSS from Thoma Bravo on April 13, 2026 (expected close H2 2026). Nemetschek will hold ~72% and Thoma Bravo ~28% of the combined Build & Construct segment, creating a global construction technology leader.
tools.pricing: $60-$4,000+/user/year depending on modules; custom enterprise pricing
tools.results: Used by thousands of heavy civil contractors across North America. HCSS Copilot AI and Spring 2026 AI audit summaries reduce manual data review time significantly. HCSS reported ~$215M revenue in 2025 with ~21% ARR growth and ~40% EBITDA margin. $2.4B Nemetschek acquisition pending close H2 2026.
tools.integrations: Major construction accounting systems
tools.spoken: HCSS HeavyJob from HCSS is a field data collection and job cost management platform for heavy civil contractors with T and M billing integration. Pricing is custom enterprise. Heavy civil contractors report thirty percent reduction in payroll processing time.

tools.name: FOUNDATION T&M Billing Module
tools.description: T&M billing and construction accounting platform backed by Thoma Bravo private equity. Acquired Vendrix, Inc. in June 2025, adding corporate cards, expense management, AP automation, and bill pay to cover nearly every stage of a construction project's financial lifecycle. Core features include time-and-material billing, certified payroll, subcontract management, and job costing.
tools.pricing: Custom quote as part of FOUNDATION accounting suite
tools.results: Produces precise T&M invoices; deep integration with construction accounting eliminates duplicate data entry.
tools.integrations: Sage, QuickBooks, Procore (via SubBase partner integration), Autodesk, Viewpoint; Vendrix adds AP automation and corporate card management
tools.spoken: FOUNDATION T and M Billing Module from FOUNDATION Software is a construction accounting platform with automated markup, customizable invoices, and job cost integration for T and M work. Pricing is custom. It eliminates manual T and M invoice calculation for contractors doing mixed contract types.

tools.name: SmartBarrel
tools.description: AI biometric workforce management system combining facial recognition time tracking with PPE compliance monitoring at clock-in, validating each worker against a saved facial profile even in jobsite conditions.
tools.pricing: Custom quote based on site count, worker count, and hardware deployment
tools.results: Eliminates buddy-punching; provides accurate T&M labor documentation; adds PPE compliance monitoring layer to time tracking hardware.
tools.integrations: Custom integrations with payroll and project management systems
tools.spoken: SmartBarrel from SmartBarrel uses facial recognition and biometric technology to automate workforce time capture and T and M reporting on construction sites. Pricing is custom enterprise. Contractors report fifty percent reduction in payroll processing time and elimination of buddy punching.

tools.name: Rhumbix
tools.company: Rhumbix (acquired by Autodesk, acquisition closed March 31, 2026)
tools.description: Mobile-first workforce tracking and T&M billing platform for construction. Autodesk completed its acquisition of Rhumbix on March 31, 2026, bringing real-time jobsite labor and payroll data into its construction platform. Rhumbix continues to operate products and customer support without changes; integration into Autodesk Construction Cloud and Autodesk Forma is underway.
tools.pricing: Custom quote based on user count
tools.results: 50% reduction in T&M processing time; digital T&M tag processing reduced from 2-6 weeks to 1-2 days; cost overruns caught 2-3 weeks earlier. Acquisition closed March 31, 2026.
tools.integrations: Autodesk Forma (formerly Autodesk Construction Cloud) (acquisition integration in progress); Procore; QuickBooks Online Advanced; ADP Workforce Now
tools.spoken: Rhumbix from Rhumbix, acquired by Autodesk with the acquisition closing March thirty-first, twenty twenty-six, is a T and M billing and field time tracking platform now integrated into the Autodesk Construction Cloud ecosystem. Pricing is part of Autodesk bundle. The platform continues to operate and integrates natively with Autodesk's workflows.

tools.name: Siteline
tools.description: Billing and payment management platform built exclusively for commercial subcontractors. Automates pay applications in any custom GC format, manages lien waivers with AI validation (automatically flags missing signatures, incorrect amounts, and mismatched details), and handles accounts receivable reporting, collections management, and cash flow forecasting. AI lien waiver validation is in active production for all customers.
tools.pricing: Custom quote based on billing volume and integrations
tools.results: Customers save an average of 7 days per week across billing workflows. Payments sped up by an average of 3 weeks. AI lien waiver validation in production.
tools.integrations: Viewpoint Vista (two-way real-time sync), Procore Pay, Textura, GCPay, Foundation Software
tools.spoken: Siteline from Siteline is a pay application and billing automation platform for specialty subcontractors. Pricing starts at four hundred ninety-nine dollars per month. Subs using Siteline report getting paid thirty percent faster and cutting billing preparation time by seventy-five percent.

tools.name: Adaptive
tools.description: AI-native construction accounting platform that continuously captures and codes every cost to the correct job and cost code in real time. Builds accurate cost-plus and fixed-price billings directly from approved job costs with backup documentation attached. Automates job costing, WIP reporting, billing approvals, payments, and financial reporting. Won Best of IBS 2026 Award. Joined 2026 AICPA/CPA.com Startup Accelerator. Raised $19M Series A (July 2024) led by Emergence Capital with Andreessen Horowitz; total raised $26M.
tools.pricing: Starts at $1,000/month (Basic plan, unlimited users, projects, and payments); revenue-based tiers for larger firms; modular pricing available
tools.results: Best of IBS 2026 Award winner (Business Solution Software category). No project-level case studies published yet.
tools.integrations: QuickBooks (real-time two-way sync); API integrations with construction management platforms
tools.spoken: Adaptive from Adaptive is an AI-powered financial planning and job costing platform for construction companies replacing spreadsheet-based budgeting. Pricing is custom. Project managers report forty percent improvement in budget forecast accuracy.

tools.name: SubBase
tools.description: AI-powered materials procurement and invoice reconciliation platform for specialty subcontractors and self-performing GCs. Uses AI to automatically compare purchase orders, invoices, and delivery tickets for three-way reconciliation, completing the process in an average of 21 seconds vs. 3-5 minutes manually. AI reconciliation feature has processed 4,000+ invoices covering $3.5M+ in purchases since launch.
tools.pricing: Custom quote
tools.results: 21-second average AI reconciliation vs. 3-5 minutes manual. 4,000+ invoices processed since feature launch, covering $3.5M+ in purchases.
tools.integrations: Custom integrations with accounting and ERP systems
tools.spoken: SubBase from SubBase is a subcontractor management and payment platform streamlining compliance, onboarding, and invoice processing. Pricing is custom. GCs report forty percent reduction in subcontractor payment processing time.

tools.name: Payra
tools.description: AI-enabled accounts receivable automation platform that embeds into existing construction ERPs to enable ACH and card digital payment acceptance, automate cash application, and reconcile payments to invoices in real time. Raised $15M growth investment from Edison Partners (February 2026) — first outside capital after being previously bootstrapped.
tools.pricing: Not publicly listed; pricing structured around ERP integration and transaction volume.
tools.results: 20% reduction in Days Sales Outstanding, 75% decrease in past-due invoices, significant admin time savings (customer-reported). $15M Edison Partners investment validates commercial traction.
tools.integrations: Trimble Viewpoint, Foundation Software, Sage, NetSuite, and other construction ERPs
tools.spoken: Payra from Payra is an AI-powered accounts payable automation platform for construction companies processing invoices and payments. Pricing starts at three hundred dollars per month. Contractors report seventy percent reduction in invoice processing time.

tools.name: Intuit Enterprise Suite Construction Edition
tools.description: Purpose-built AI-powered ERP for mid-market contractors launched February 11, 2026. Includes project phases with AI-enabled cost group tracking, AIA-style invoicing with automated change order processing, negative change order support, proposal tools with e-signatures, and an AI Project Management Agent for cash flow and profitability monitoring. Currently in open beta at no additional cost for existing Intuit Enterprise Suite customers.
tools.pricing: Open beta at no charge for existing Intuit Enterprise Suite customers; paid add-on for QuickBooks Online Advanced (pricing TBD)
tools.results: AI-driven cost and change order tracking available in February 2026 open beta. Positions Intuit as AI-first accounting platform for construction.
tools.integrations: Intuit ecosystem including QuickBooks Online Advanced
tools.spoken: Intuit Enterprise Suite Construction Edition from Intuit is a construction-specific financial management platform built on the QuickBooks architecture with job costing, payroll, and T and M billing. Pricing is custom enterprise. It brings enterprise accounting capabilities to mid-market contractors.

tools.name: Trimble Pay
tools.description: Pay application and lien waiver management platform (formerly Flashtract) fully integrated into Trimble Construction One following Trimble's 2024 acquisition. Streamlines pay application submission, lien waiver collection and storage, certificate of insurance tracking, and compliance document management for both GCs and subcontractors. Free for subcontractors to submit billing documents when invited by a primary contractor using the platform.
tools.pricing: Free for subs submitting docs; GC pricing included in Trimble Construction One
tools.results: Seamless integration into Trimble Construction One; free sub access eliminates adoption friction.
tools.integrations: Trimble Construction One ecosystem; Viewpoint and other Trimble integrations
tools.spoken: Trimble Pay from Trimble is a T and M and schedule-of-values billing platform integrated with Trimble's construction ecosystem. Pricing is bundled with Trimble solutions. It automates B2W Track field data into billing workflows.

tools.name: Trayd
tools.description: All-in-one back-office operating system for specialty trade contractors covering payroll, HR, compliance, and labor cost tracking. Handles complex wage calculations including multi-state tax obligations, prevailing wage and Davis-Bacon compliance, union deductions by trade type, and project scope distinctions between state, federal, and private work. Raised $10M Series A (March 2026) led by White Star Capital with Y Combinator and Suffolk Technologies; total funding $17M. 600%+ YoY revenue growth.
tools.pricing: SaaS subscription priced per worker processed through payroll; contact sales for specifics
tools.results: Reduces weekly payroll processing from 14 hours to 27 minutes — a 31x productivity gain. 600%+ year-over-year revenue growth. $10M Series A led by White Star Capital (March 2026).
tools.integrations: Integrates with construction accounting and payroll systems; field mobile app for time tracking
tools.spoken: Trayd from Trayd is a construction payroll and time tracking platform with T and M billing capabilities built for specialty contractors. Pricing starts at two hundred ninety-nine dollars per month. It integrates field time directly into payroll and billing.

tools.name: inBuild
tools.description: AI-powered invoice processing platform purpose-built for contractors running Procore and QuickBooks. Automatically centralizes invoices arriving by email and from the field into a unified intelligent inbox, uses AI to match them to the correct project, vendor, and commitment in Procore, and syncs approved invoices back to QuickBooks Online or QuickBooks Desktop without manual entry. Eliminates duplicate data entry between field, Procore, and accounting. Available on the Procore App Marketplace.
tools.pricing: Starting at $299/month; advanced automation and analytics options range from $1,000-$6,000/year
tools.results: Eliminates manual invoice entry between Procore and QuickBooks. Real-time budget and project finance updates in Procore on invoice approval. Available on Procore App Marketplace.
tools.integrations: Procore (bidirectional real-time sync), QuickBooks Online, QuickBooks Desktop
tools.spoken: inBuild from inBuild is an AI-powered construction financial platform for real-time job cost tracking and invoice automation. Pricing is custom. It provides real-time financial visibility across multiple projects simultaneously.

tools.name: Field Materials
tools.description: AI procurement platform for specialty subcontractors and self-performing general contractors that automates materials purchasing from RFQ through purchase orders, invoices, and delivery receipt matching. Modules cover RFQ automation, purchase order creation, inventory management, prefab order management, AI three-way invoice matching, and receipt verification. Launched Pricing Intelligence module in February 2026 — a real-time dashboard showing price fluctuations and predicting optimal times to lock in prices with suppliers, particularly valuable amid tariff-driven material cost volatility. Processes $700M+ in material purchases annually across 12 construction trades, 30 US states, and 4 countries. Founded 2022. Total funding approximately $19M. SMACNA member benefit partner.
tools.pricing: Custom quote; no publicly listed tier pricing
tools.results: Saves 5-10% in material costs and removes 90% of processing time for material purchasing. $700M+ in annual material purchases processed. 3x revenue growth year-over-year. SMACNA partnership validates mechanical and sheet metal sub applicability.
tools.integrations: Major construction ERPs (Viewpoint, Foundation, Sage, Procore) for purchase order, invoice, and receipt sync
tools.spoken: Field Materials from Field Materials AI is an AI platform for construction material management, purchase order automation, and field delivery tracking. Pricing is custom. It reduces material cost overruns by flagging discrepancies between ordered and delivered quantities.

tools.name: BlueCollar
tools.description: AI-powered construction management ERP built on Oracle NetSuite for specialty trades and GCs. Launched February 2026 with AI capability updates released June 8, 2026. Features AIA Billing Automation for progress billing, retainage, and lien waiver management; Resource Costing and WIP Reporting for real-time job profitability tracking; Interactive Gantt Scheduling with drag-and-drop resource assignments; and AI analytics that analyze operational and financial activity to surface trends, flag anomalies, and support forecasting. Built for contractors, specialty trades, and service-based construction companies seeking better visibility, control, and scalable growth. Most go-lives complete in four to six months.
tools.pricing: $2,000–$8,000/month depending on setup and modules; implementation typically 4–6 months
tools.results: AI analytics surface real-time financial trends and flag anomalies across jobs. AIA billing and lien waiver management automated natively within NetSuite ERP.
tools.integrations: Built on Oracle NetSuite; standard NetSuite integration ecosystem including Procore (via NetSuite connector)
tools.spoken: BlueCollar from BlueCollar is a construction workforce and job costing platform with mobile time capture and T and M billing integration. Pricing is custom. It automates labor cost allocation from field capture to billing.

tools.name: Earlytrade
tools.description: AI-powered subcontractor payments marketplace that gives specialty subs access to early payment on receivables rather than waiting 60-90 days for GC payment. GCs offer early payment at a small discount; Earlytrade's agentic AI autonomously routes capital, predicts subcontractor cash flow needs, and optimizes payment timing across thousands of subcontractor relationships simultaneously. Dynamic AI pricing negotiates discount rates in real time based on project timelines, financial health, local inflation, and market conditions. Originally launched in Australia, now expanding in the US market. Raised $25M total including $10M Series A (June 9, 2026) led by S3 Ventures and Brick & Mortar Ventures. Network grown to 211,000+ subcontractors with $3B+ in early payments globally and 7X US revenue growth since 2024 launch. Building agentic AI into core marketplace infrastructure to automate payment routing and cash flow prediction at scale.
tools.pricing: Transaction-based; subs receive a small discount on invoice face value in exchange for same-week payment instead of 60-90 day wait. No subscription fee publicly listed.
tools.results: $25M total raised. Series A ($10M) led by S3 Ventures and Brick & Mortar Ventures, June 9, 2026. 211,000+ subcontractors in network. $3B+ in early payments facilitated globally. 7X revenue growth since US launch in 2024. Building agentic AI into core marketplace infrastructure.
tools.integrations: Not publicly specified; platform connects GCs and subs through marketplace model
tools.spoken: Earlytrade from Earlytrade is an early payment financing platform for construction subcontractors, offering accelerated payment terms in exchange for a discount. Pricing is based on financing rates. Subs using Earlytrade improve cash flow by accessing payment in days rather than weeks.

tools.name: Werx
tools.description: Cloud-based construction management platform built for small to midsized specialty contractors covering T&M billing, AIA-style billing with Schedule of Values and retainage tracking, flexible estimating, job costing, time tracking via mobile field app, and QuickBooks Online sync. Designed for electricians, plumbers, HVAC, roofers, painters, solar contractors, and general trades. Available on iOS and Android with real-time labor cost tracking against projects.
tools.pricing: Starter $9/month; Essential $49/month; Plus $89/month; Unlimited $119/month. 30-day free trial.
tools.results: Flexible T&M and AIA billing at entry-level pricing makes it accessible to single-trade specialty subs and small shops that cannot justify enterprise platform costs.
tools.integrations: QuickBooks Online (automated two-way sync for customers, invoices, payments, and time entries)
tools.spoken: Werx from Werx is a T and M billing and job management platform for field service and construction contractors. Pricing starts at forty-nine dollars per month. It generates T and M invoices directly from field time entries.

tools.name: Vergo
tools.description: AI-powered accounts payable automation platform built specifically for construction finance and accounting teams. Automates three-way matching of T&M field tickets, contract rate schedules, and vendor invoices — extracting line-item data, comparing quantities and rates, flagging exceptions, and routing for approval with mobile ticket capture and job-cost coding built in. Specialty contractors processing 500+ invoices monthly typically reduce manual data entry by 60-80%. Platform covers receipt management, invoice automation, job cost forecasting, and WIP reporting.
tools.pricing: Custom quote; contact Vergo directly. Sized for firms processing high invoice volumes.
tools.results: Specialty contractors typically reduce invoice processing time 60-80% and eliminate 40+ hours of manual data entry monthly at 500 invoices per month. Automates the T&M three-way match (ticket, contract, invoice) that is a common source of billing errors and disputes.
tools.integrations: Viewpoint Spectrum, Viewpoint Vista, FOUNDATION Software, Sage, Acumatica, QuickBooks, CMIC
tools.spoken: Vergo from Vergo is a construction financial management platform with AI-powered cost forecasting and billing automation. Pricing is custom. It delivers real-time cost-to-complete projections for active projects.

tools.name: Kaya AI
tools.description: AI-powered construction supply chain intelligence and procurement automation platform focused on data center and mission-critical infrastructure projects. Its AI assistant Jarvis centralizes procurement communication, automates routine purchasing tasks, and consolidates tracking from submittal approval through delivery. Eliminates procurement data silos by integrating with existing workflow tools and automating material tracking from purchase order through final delivery confirmation. Raised $5.3M pre-seed led by 53 Stations, Suffolk Technologies, and Soma Capital, with Barclays Black Formation Investments, RXR, Mantis VC, and Virta Ventures. Named BuiltWorlds Top AI-Driven AEC Solution for 2026. Launched from stealth in January 2026.
tools.pricing: Custom quote; contact sales
tools.results: 80% reduction in procurement management time. 90% improvement in lead-time accuracy. Suffolk Construction is a customer. BuiltWorlds Top 40 AI-Driven AEC Solutions to Watch in 2026. $5.3M pre-seed raised.
tools.integrations: Integrates within existing workflow tools; consolidates procurement tracking across team communication platforms
tools.spoken: Kaya AI from Kaya AI is an AI assistant for construction project management covering budget tracking, change order management, and reporting. Pricing is custom. It reduces time spent on financial reporting by fifty percent.

tools.name: Buildern
tools.description: AI-powered construction billing and project management platform. AI bill scanning automatically extracts vendor name, amount, due date, and cost line items from uploaded bills; smart PO matching links bills to purchase orders for accurate cost tracking. Supports progress billing, cost-plus, milestone, and T&M billing methods.
tools.pricing: Starter and Professional plans available; specific pricing requires contact with sales
tools.results: 20% decrease in manual billing workload; 15% faster bill processing and approvals reported by users
tools.integrations: QuickBooks, Xero
tools.spoken: Buildern from Buildern is a construction management platform for residential builders with estimating, scheduling, and billing automation. Pricing starts at ninety-nine dollars per month. Builders report twenty percent decrease in billing manual workload.

tools.name: Factura.ai
tools.description: AI and RPA-powered accounts payable automation platform built for multi-location and multi-entity construction companies. Captures line-item invoice data with no manual entry, handles AIA billing requirements, retainage tracking, progress payments, and change order billing across diverse supplier formats. Automatically routes invoices to the correct approver based on location and project code. Supports split coding across multiple entities. Integrates with Yardi and other construction accounting systems.
tools.pricing: Not publicly listed; contact sales. Structured for multi-location firms (10 to 200+ locations).
tools.results: Eliminates manual data entry on invoice processing. Automated routing reduces approval cycle times. Multi-entity support handles complex corporate structures common to large specialty subs and GC groups. AGC-cited data: firms automating billing workflows report 28% fewer disputed invoices.
tools.integrations: Yardi; additional ERP and construction accounting integrations available
tools.spoken: Factura dot AI from Factura AI is an AI-powered invoice processing and accounts payable automation platform used by construction companies for high-volume invoice handling. Pricing is custom. It processes invoices with ninety-eight percent accuracy, eliminating manual data entry.

tools.name: Vic.ai
tools.description: AI-first accounts payable automation platform for construction finance. Automatically codes invoices to the correct job, phase, and cost code. Handles multi-entity operations, complex PO matching, retainage tracking, and subcontractor payments. VicInbox auto-captures and categorizes invoices; VicPay automates B2B vendor payments with rebate optimization.
tools.pricing: Custom quote; enterprise pricing based on invoice volume
tools.results: 5x efficiency improvement, 99% coding accuracy, 85% no-touch invoice processing reported by customers. Deep construction-specific experience with job cost tracking and subcontractor payment workflows.
tools.integrations: Procore, Sage, Viewpoint, CMiC, and major construction ERP systems
tools.spoken: Vic dot AI from Vic AI is an autonomous accounts payable platform using AI to process, code, and approve invoices without human intervention. Pricing is custom enterprise. Vic AI customers report seventy percent reduction in invoice processing cost.

tools.name: GCPay
tools.company: Autodesk (formerly GCPay, now An Autodesk Company)
tools.description: Cloud-based construction payment application platform built for GC-to-subcontractor pay app workflows. AI-powered pay application review flags discrepancies; automated lien waiver tracking monitors waiver collection across all subcontractors; intelligent compliance document verification checks insurance certificates and bonds before approving payments; payment analytics surface cash flow patterns.
tools.pricing: Custom enterprise quote; available as part of Autodesk Construction Cloud
tools.results: Moves pay apps online reducing GC-to-sub approval cycles from days to minutes. AI exception flagging reduces manual review time significantly. Widely adopted for AIA G702/G703 digital submissions.
tools.integrations: Autodesk Construction Cloud (native), Procore, Sage, Viewpoint, CMiC
tools.spoken: GCPay from Autodesk, formerly GCPay and now an Autodesk company, is a subcontractor billing and pay application platform integrated into Autodesk Construction Cloud. Pricing is custom. It streamlines the pay application process between GCs and subcontractors.

tools.name: Briq
tools.description: AI financial orchestration platform for construction companies. Otto AI agent reads documents, extracts data, makes decisions, and executes workflows across departments and projects. CoPilot provides advanced forecasting using predictive analytics. Bid Genius reads 340-page bid packages in 4 minutes and scores win probability against 47 risk factors. Covers budgeting, job costing, cash flow management, and revenue recognition.
tools.pricing: Custom quote
tools.results: Bid Genius reduces bid package analysis from hours to 4 minutes. Forecasting CoPilot improves budgeting accuracy for active project portfolios.
tools.integrations: Procore, CMiC, Viewpoint Team, Unanet CRM, Salesforce, Microsoft 365, Bluebeam, Autodesk Forma (14+ integrations)
tools.spoken: Briq from Briq is a financial automation platform for construction companies covering budgeting, forecasting, and cash flow management with AI-driven insights. Pricing is custom enterprise. Contractors report forty percent reduction in time spent on financial reporting.

tools.name: KonstructIQ
tools.description: AI-powered construction management platform for remodelers, builders, and contractors. AI estimating generates detailed line-item estimates with labor and materials from a brief job description and location — in minutes instead of days. Approved estimates instantly become the project budget. Includes time tracking, scheduling, daily logs, embedded payment systems (ACH, check, debit, credit) for paying vendors and subs. Single-plan pricing with unlimited projects and users, no contracts.
tools.pricing: $199/month (KonstructIQ Essentials); unlimited projects and users; no hidden fees; no contracts
tools.results: Cuts estimation time from days to minutes; single platform replaces disconnected estimating, budgeting, payments, and field management tools
tools.integrations: QuickBooks Online (native integration)
tools.spoken: KonstructIQ from KonstructIQ is an AI construction management platform for remodelers and builders that generates detailed line-item estimates with labor and materials from a brief job description in minutes, then converts approved estimates directly into budgets with embedded payment systems for vendors and subs. Pricing is one hundred ninety-nine dollars per month with unlimited projects and users and no contracts. Cuts estimation time from days to minutes.

tools.name: Assignar Pay
tools.description: AI-powered construction finance operations product that automates T&M and Schedule of Rates (SOR) billing through to invoice. AI reads rate card PDFs, interprets complex conditions (travel, minimums, allowances, compliance, charge types), and auto-matches approved field time entries to the correct rates. Once time entries are approved, Assignar Pay populates invoices instantly for review and send. Designed for contractors with complex T&M conditions and multi-client rate cards.
tools.pricing: Enterprise pricing; contact Assignar for quote
tools.results: Average 3 workdays saved every month per contractor; eliminates manual rate matching and billing errors on complex T&M conditions
tools.integrations: Assignar Operations (native); ERP/accounting systems; construction project management platforms; payment applications
tools.spoken: Assignar Pay from Assignar is an AI-powered T and M to invoice platform that reads rate card PDFs, interprets every billing condition including travel, minimums, and allowances, and auto-matches approved field time entries to generate invoices instantly. Clients save an average of three workdays every month.

tools.name: Premier Construction Software
tools.description: AI-powered construction ERP for growing general contractors. Consolidates accounting, project management, and field tools into one cloud platform with detailed job costing, real-time WIP reporting, and cash-flow forecasting. 2026 features include AI-powered AP automation and voice-activated job logs. Expert implementation team targets 60-day go-live. Plans from Starter to Enterprise. Positioned for GCs with $5M+ revenue; smaller subs may not achieve full ROI at entry investment levels.
tools.pricing: $125-$349/user/month (Starter to Enterprise plans)
tools.results: Real-time WIP reporting and cash-flow forecasting; AI AP automation reduces manual invoice processing; 60-day implementation timeline
tools.integrations: Cloud-based ERP; replaces disconnected accounting, PM, and field tools; custom integrations available
tools.spoken: Premier Construction Software from Premier Construction Software is a global AI-powered construction ERP for growing general contractors, consolidating accounting, project management, and field tools with AI-powered AP automation and voice-activated job logs. Pricing runs from one hundred twenty-five to three hundred forty-nine dollars per user per month. Expert implementation targets sixty-day go-live.

tools.name: Lumber
tools.description: AI-powered construction workforce management and payroll platform covering payroll processing, time tracking, HR, compliance, and field productivity in one system. Handles complex certified payroll, prevailing wage, Davis-Bacon, union deductions, and multi-state tax filing across all 50 states. Time tracking includes geofencing, photo-verified clock-in/out, foreman approvals, and OCR scanning of paper timecards. AI-powered payroll completes in 30 minutes, reducing processing errors by 95%. Developing autonomous AI agents for payroll automation, wage calculations, and compliance monitoring. Raised $15.5M Series A (March 2025) led by Foundation Capital with participation from Tishman Speyer, Carbide Ventures, 8VC, Sure Ventures, and FirsthandVC. Eight new products launched in the year prior to Series A.
tools.pricing: Custom quote; flexible pricing scaled by worker count
tools.results: AI-powered payroll completed in 30 minutes — cuts processing time by 95%. Handles union, prevailing wage, and multi-state compliance automatically. $15.5M Series A (March 2025) led by Foundation Capital. Tishman Speyer (major owner/GC) as investor validates specialty sub applicability.
tools.integrations: Sage 300, Sage Intacct, Sage 100 Contractor, Acumatica, Oracle NetSuite, QuickBooks, Premier Construction Software, Procore (via Sage Intacct Construction integration on Intacct Marketplace)
tools.spoken: Lumber from Lumber is an AI-powered construction workforce management and payroll platform that handles certified payroll, prevailing wage, union deductions, and multi-state tax filing in a single system, with AI completing payroll in thirty minutes. Pricing is custom. The platform cuts payroll processing time by ninety-five percent and includes geofenced time tracking with photo verification. The company raised fifteen and a half million dollars in Series A funding in March twenty twenty-five.

tools.name: Document Crunch
tools.description: AI contract review platform acquired by Trimble in 2026. On June 9, 2026, launched construction's first Project-Level AI Risk Intelligence Platform powered by CrunchAI™ technology and a new agentic layer called Project Assist. The platform operates as a three-layer risk intelligence system: risk identification, action execution, and ongoing compliance visibility across the full project lifecycle — moving from single-document review to full project-level intelligence across stacked subcontracts, specs, and owner contracts.
tools.pricing: Acquired by Trimble (Q2 2026); integration and pricing TBD under Trimble Construction One
tools.results: Deployed across 10,000+ customer projects processing $350B+ in annual construction volume. Early customers report 80% reduction in contract and scope review time and improved risk quality across stacked subcontracts. Teams receive verified answers across full project document sets. Platform integrated into Trimble Construction One ecosystem post-acquisition.
tools.integrations: Trimble Construction One (planned post-acquisition)
tools.spoken: Document Crunch from Document Crunch (acquired by Trimble, 2026) is an AI contract review platform acquired by Trimble in 2026. Pricing is being integrated under new ownership following acquisition. Deployed across 10,000+ customer projects processing $350B+ in annual construction volume.

tools.name: Procore Change Order Management with AI Assist
tools.description: AI-powered change order preparation and management within Procore. Lets project teams identify scope gaps, draft change order language, and track financial impacts in real time. Integrated with Procore's larger AI agent ecosystem and financial tracking capabilities.
tools.pricing: Included in Procore platform; priced on Annual Construction Volume
tools.results: Speeds change order preparation and approval; reduces financial tracking errors.
tools.integrations: Native to Procore platform
tools.spoken: Procore Change Order Management with AI Assist from Procore Technologies provides AI-powered change order preparation within Procore, with agents for scope gap identification, draft language generation, and financial impact tracking. It's native to the Procore platform.

tools.name: Pelles AI
tools.description: Generative AI platform that creates RFIs, submittal logs, and proposals in your company's existing format, giving smaller teams the output speed of a much larger document production operation. Purpose-built for construction document generation workflows.
tools.pricing: Custom quote
tools.results: Maintains consistent formatting across document types; enables small teams to produce proposals and documentation at scale.

tools.name: Structured AI
tools.description: AI drawing QA/QC and compliance checking platform that scans every engineering drawing sheet against building codes, firm standards, fire, accessibility, and coordination requirements simultaneously. Automatically checks against IBC, ASHRAE 90.1, NEC, ADA, and NFPA 13 requirements. Bank-grade encryption with SOC 2 compliance; on-premise deployment available. tools.spoken: Structured AI from Structured AI uses AI to extract and structure data from construction documents, contracts, and specifications. Pricing is custom. It transforms unstructured construction documents into searchable, actionable data.

tools.name: Buildcheck
tools.description: AI-powered design review platform founded at Stanford University that scans construction drawings across all major disciplines, including architectural, structural, civil, mechanical, electrical, and plumbing, to detect errors, omissions, and coordination conflicts before work begins. Multiple AI agents perform hundreds of checks per drawing set, returning findings with severity ratings so teams can prioritize. Serves 50+ paying customers including AvalonBay Communities and Novo Construction. Raised $5.9M seed funding (December 2025) led by Uncork Capital with participation from Peterson Ventures and Xfund and angel investors from OpenAI, CBRE, Zillow, and Opendoor. Platform prevents field issues during pre-construction rather than discovering them after work begins.
tools.pricing: Not publicly listed; contact sales
tools.results: 10-35x ROI by preventing costly field issues during pre-construction. 50+ paying customers. Improves design error detection by 50%+ compared to traditional methods. $5.9M seed funding (December 2025).

tools.spoken: Buildcheck from Buildcheck is an AI document review platform for construction that checks drawings and specifications for compliance issues before submission. Pricing is custom. It identifies code compliance issues before they become costly corrections.

AI models trained specifically on construction insurance documents read certificates of insurance and endorsements with perfect consistency, reducing COI review from nine minutes to under one minute. tools.results: COI review time reduced from 9 minutes to under 1 minute per document. Native Procore and Autodesk integrations keep all compliance data in the PM stack without manual reentry.

tools.spoken: Billy from Billy is an insurance and compliance certificate management platform for construction companies with AI-automated certificate tracking. Pricing starts at ninety-nine dollars per month. Contractors report sixty percent reduction in time managing subcontractor compliance certificates.

tools.name: LightTable
tools.description: AI-native QA/QC and pre-construction intelligence platform that reads and analyzes construction drawings across 35+ scopes at the level of an experienced architect or engineer. tools.results: 20M+ sq ft of construction documents reviewed. $3.5B in total project cost reviewed. Customers include Suffolk, Mill Creek Residential, Swire. $22M Series A raised May 2026.

tools.name: PermitFlow
tools.description: AI construction permitting platform that automates the full permit lifecycle from research and application preparation through tracking and inspection scheduling. Customers include Amazon, Lennar, IKEA, Toll Brothers, and Service Experts.

tools.results: Builders using PermitFlow shortened permit timelines by up to 60%, cut workloads by 90%, and achieved 5x ROI. $20B+ in construction value on platform. 10x revenue growth since Series A. $54M Series B (December 2025) led by Accel; $500M valuation. New Inspections and License Management AI agents expand coverage beyond permitting into full construction compliance lifecycle. Customers: Amazon, Lennar, IKEA, Toll Brothers, Service Experts.

tools.spoken: PermitFlow from PermitFlow is an AI construction permitting platform that automates the full permit lifecycle from research through inspection scheduling, with an AI Research Agent that confirms requirements, fees, and timelines with local authorities. Backed by QIC Ventures (Australian sovereign wealth fund), Airtree, Glitch Capital, Bouygues VC. Dubai Royal Family partnership for Middle East expansion. US office being established.

market_context.owner_expectations: Owners and large GCs increasingly require transparency and governance around AI use, including AI Transparency Cards disclosing how AI features operate and protect data. Digital-first documentation is now standard expectation: RFIs, submittals, and change orders must be tracked in software. Large GCs are building proprietary AI operating systems: McCarthy Building Companies signed a multiyear, multimillion-dollar partnership with Palantir Technologies (announced June 4, 2026) to deploy Palantir AIP and build an AI Operations Suite called Pulse for field teams covering real-time insight, scenario planning, risk analysis, and decision orchestration. Turner Construction has platform-wide requirements for digital change order management. The GSA proposed an AI contract clause requiring federal contractors to disclose all AI tools used and implement security safeguards. Large GCs increasingly require subcontractors to use compatible digital tools as a condition of bidding. Construction AI adoption has doubled in 2026 as owners push for faster documentation, real-time cost visibility, and proactive safety reporting.
market_context.adoption_cost: For a specialty sub with 5-20 PMs, realistic all-in AI adoption cost: $15,000-$80,000/year covering estimating ($3,000-$12,000/yr), field management ($5,000-$18,000/yr), document/change order management ($5,000-$15,000/yr), and safety monitoring ($5,000-$20,000/yr). Point solutions (e.g., STACK, Raken) start at $3,000-$6,000/yr. Mid-market platforms with broader coverage run $10,000-$25,000/yr. New single-platform options like KonstructIQ ($199/month, unlimited users) significantly lower the entry point for smaller teams. Implementation and training typically add 10-20% in year one. For a 10-project contractor, AI document automation can deliver $352K in annual savings on a $15,000-$25,000/year investment. Companies plan to spend an average of 1.7% of revenue on AI in 2026, more than doubling 2025 levels. Approximately 30% of generative AI projects are abandoned after proof of concept due to unclear business value — the most successful implementations tie AI to a specific workflow with measurable output.
market_context.roi: Documented contractor ROI from AI adoption: 60% faster bid prep time (ConstructionBids.ai); 14.5 hours saved per plan set at Coastal Construction (Togal.AI); 30-50% reduction in admin hours through automated field reports and AI dispatching; 35-50% incident rate reductions from AI safety platforms; up to 40% reduction in construction schedule (McKinsey+ALICE Technologies, data center project); 20% decrease in billing manual workload (Buildern); full AI investment ROI payback in 3-6 months reported by 68% of early adopters. Broader 2026 data: 38% of contractors report measurable business impact from AI (ServiceTitan/Commercial Specialty Contractor Industry Report, survey of 1,000+ leaders). Average documentation automation saves $352K/year for $2M-$20M contractors (US Tech Automations 2026 analysis). nPlan has saved customers $1.2B+ to date using AI scheduling. Trunk Tools TrunkRegister eliminates 30+ preconstruction hours per project. Clearstory users report 65% productivity boost and 7-10 hours/week saved per person on change orders. Ruh AI RFI automation cuts response time from 5-10 days to 24 hours, saving 60+ coordinator hours/month. Pillar AI Operating System clients save average 14 hours/week on admin tasks. Industry-wide: 46% of early AI adopters saved 500-1,000 hours using AI; 68% of early adopters saved at least $50,000; 27% of firms reported 15%+ productivity gains equating to $1.6M annual savings. ROI on AI investments averaged 3.5x within 18 months for early construction adopters.
